s3.q: Which of the following is a key concept?
options: It is essential for understanding the condition
It is rarely relevant in clinical practice
It applies only to specific populations
It has been replaced by newer classifications
s3.explain: Understanding key concepts is fundamental to clinical management.

s3.q: The clinical significance of this section is:
options: It applies only to research settings
s3.explain: Understanding these concepts directly informs clinical management.

s3.q: When assessing this aspect, the most important factor is:
s3.explain: Assessment requires integration of clinical, laboratory, and imaging findings.

s3.q: In obstetric practice, this knowledge helps:
s3.explain: Comprehensive knowledge enables prevention, early diagnosis, and optimal management.

s3.q: Which of the following best defines this condition?
options: A pathological deviation from normal anatomy or function
An incidental finding with no clinical significance
s3.explain: A definition describes the essential nature of a condition as a deviation from normal.

s3.q: When does the postpartum period (puerperium) begin and how long does it last?
options: Begins after delivery of placenta and continues for 4 weeks
Begins about 2 hours after delivery of placenta and continues for 6 weeks
Begins immediately after delivery and continues for 8 weeks
Begins 24 hours after delivery and continues for 6 weeks
s3.explain: The postpartum period starts about two hours after delivery of the placenta and continues for six weeks.

s3.q: Which of the following is an objective of postnatal care for the mother?
options: Reproductive health promotion and prevention of complications
s3.explain: Objectives for the mother include: reproductive health promotion, prevention of postpartum complications, early detection and referral, psychological support, and health education.

s4.q: Which sign on examination is characteristic?
s4.explain: Examination findings depend heavily on the specific obstetric condition.

s4.q: The clinical significance of this section is:
options: It applies only to research settings
s4.explain: Understanding these concepts directly informs clinical management.

s4.q: The most common presenting symptom is:
s4.explain: Clinical presentation varies — pain and bleeding are common but not universal symptoms.

s4.q: On Day 1 postpartum, which item should be checked as given?
s4.explain: On Day 1, the nurse should check that Vitamin A capsule (200,000 IU) has been given to the mother.

s4.q: On Day 4 postpartum, which symptom should be specifically asked about?
s4.explain: On Day 4, the nurse asks about calf pain (thrombotic), chest pain, foul-smelling discharge, and fever/chills.

s5.explain: Comprehensive knowledge enables prevention, early diagnosis, and optimal management.

options: It is essential for understanding the condition

s5.explain: Understanding key concepts is fundamental to clinical management.

s5.q: The clinical significance of this section is:
options: It applies only to research settings
s5.explain: Understanding these concepts directly informs clinical management.

s5.q: What laboratory investigations are performed on Day 40 postpartum?
options: Complete blood count and liver function tests
Hemoglobin level and complete urine analysis
Thyroid function tests and vitamin D level
s5.explain: On Day 40, hemoglobin level and complete urine analysis are the recommended lab investigations.

s5.q: On Day 1 postpartum, what abdominal finding should be assessed?
options: Fundal height and firmness by palpation
s5.explain: On Day 1, fundal height and firmness by palpation and abdominal tenderness should be assessed to check uterine involution.

It applies only to specific populations
It has been replaced by newer classifications
s6.explain: Understanding key concepts is fundamental to clinical management.

s6.q: What newborn screening should be performed within the first week of life?
s6.explain: The mother should be counseled to have the infant screened for congenital hypothyroidism within the first week.

s6.q: By Day 7, what should have happened to the umbilical stump?
options: It should have fallen off or totally dried
It should be covered with antibiotic ointment
s6.explain: By Day 7, the umbilical stump should have fallen off or be totally dried.

s7.q: The clinical significance of this section is:
options: It applies only to research settings
s7.explain: Understanding these concepts directly informs clinical management.

s7.q: In obstetric practice, this knowledge helps:
s7.explain: Comprehensive knowledge enables prevention, early diagnosis, and optimal management.

s7.q: When assessing this aspect, the most important factor is:
s7.explain: Assessment requires integration of clinical, laboratory, and imaging findings.

s7.q: Which of the following is a danger symptom in the mother requiring immediate medical care?
s7.explain: Danger symptoms for the mother include: vaginal bleeding, severe headache, blurring of vision, convulsions, fever, foul discharge, painful calf muscle, chest pain, dyspnea, and fainting.

s7.q: Which of the following is a red flag in an infant requiring referral?
s7.explain: Infant red flags include: temperature 38°C, RR >60/min, flaccid/non-reactive, jaundice/cyanosis/pallor, nasal flaring, chest retraction, purulent umbilical discharge.

options: It applies only to research settings
s8.explain: Understanding these concepts directly informs clinical management.

s8.q: When assessing this aspect, the most important factor is:
s8.explain: Assessment requires integration of clinical, laboratory, and imaging findings.

s8.q: In obstetric practice, this knowledge helps:
s8.explain: Comprehensive knowledge enables prevention, early diagnosis, and optimal management.

s8.q: When should contraception counseling be provided to postpartum mothers?
s8.explain: Counseling about contraception should be done at Day 7 visit.

s8.q: Which of the following is a health education message for the mother?
options: Avoid breastfeeding for the first week
s8.explain: Health education includes: encourage early ambulation, successful breastfeeding, good nutrition, abdominal exercises, contraception counseling, and day 40 visit.

s9.q: The clinical significance of this section is:
options: It applies only to research settings
s9.explain: Understanding these concepts directly informs clinical management.

s9.q: For home deliveries, on which days does the nurse visit?
s9.explain: For home deliveries, nurse visits on Day 1 (within 24 hours), Day 4, and Day 7. Then mother visits center on Day 40.

s9.q: What is the purpose of the Day 40 visit?
options: Complete assessment and initiation of contraception
s9.explain: On Day 40, the mother visits the health facility for complete assessment and initiation of contraception.

s10.q: According to the postpartum care flowchart, what happens if a risk factor is detected?
options: Refer to a higher level of health care facility
s10.explain: If any risk factor (maternal or neonatal) is detected, the patient should be referred to a higher level of health care facility.

s10.q: What is the first step in the postpartum care protocol flowchart?
options: Postpartum care visits (home: Days 1, 4, 7; clinic: Day 40)
s10.explain: The first step is scheduling and conducting postpartum care visits: home visits on Days 1, 4, 7 and clinic visit on Day 40.
